content: The Next Web https://thenextweb.com Original and proudly opinionated perspectives for Generation T Wed, 24 Jun 2026 14:06:35 +0000 en-US hourly 1 TNW-API Apple’s suppliers are racing to Hong Kong to fund their robot pivot https://thenextweb.com/news/apple-suppliers-luxshare-lingyi-hong-kong-listings Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:57:08 +0000 TheNextWeb=0e0c7085f5864697d87a7e942689133a Two of Apple’s biggest Chinese suppliers are racing to Hong Kong in the same week. Luxshare wants about $3bn, Lingyi just banked $1.1bn. Both are using the cash to pivot from smartphone parts to AI hardware and humanoid robots. The companies that build your AirPods are quietly raising a fortune. The contradiction is the point. As the internet fills with machine-made text, proving something is human is becoming a product. There is a neat irony at the centre of this deal. Superhuman’s biggest product helps people write with […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Hadrian’s reported $7.5bn round and the physical-AI rush https://thenextweb.com/news/hadrian-7-5bn-valuation-physical-ai-funding Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:27:50 +0000 TheNextWeb=8c10c037c07adb9177b3f68d4936fc99 Bloomberg says defense-factory startup Hadrian is in talks to raise as much as $1bn at a $7.5bn valuation. Hadrian calls that “inaccurate”. Either way, the rumour shows how hot physical AI and US reindustrialisation have become. Here is a number, and a denial, in the same breath. Hadrian Automation has discussed a new funding round […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Main Capital raised the Netherlands’ biggest-ever fund on boring software https://thenextweb.com/news/main-capital-5-25bn-enterprise-software-funds Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:07:58 +0000 TheNextWeb=3f366c879a71e9ba217568f1d2a0a8c5 While public markets panic that AI will gut enterprise software, a quiet Dutch firm just raised €5.25bn on the opposite bet. Main Capital’s pitch: boring, essential software is exactly where the next decade of value sits. Charly Zwemstra has spent 23 years buying software that almost nobody finds exciting. Think hospital appointment systems, municipal tax […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Menlo Ventures raises $3bn on the back of one Anthropic bet https://thenextweb.com/news/menlo-ventures-3bn-anthropic-ai-funds Wed, 24 Jun 2026 12:58:09 +0000 TheNextWeb=63fe3abe20ab541bdcf44e28c691644d Menlo Ventures has raised $3bn, the biggest haul in its 50-year history. It is funded, in effect, by a single bet on Anthropic that is now worth about $14bn. Most venture funds are sold on a promise. Menlo Ventures’ new $3bn is sold on a receipt. The price tells you how far retailers will now go to catch Amazon in advertising. Walmart has agreed to buy Vibe.co, a connected-TV advertising platform. Two reports peg the deal at $1.4bn. The harder question is whether a deeply unprofitable lab can turn the moment into a durable business. Six months ago, Zhipu was a Chinese artificial-intelligence lab fresh off a $558M Hong Kong listing.
